perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a close-up of a young woman enthusiastically eating a bowl of orange, cheesy porridge; the cheese stretches significantly as she lifts a spoonful to her mouth. (2) The scene shifts to the woman sitting at a table, holding a packet of Samyang Buldak Ramen and explaining ingredients. (3) A series of quick cuts follows, showing her preparing the dish: cutting noodles with scissors in an electric pot, adding cooked rice, pouring in spicy sauce, and adding water. (4) She then sprinkles a large amount of shredded cheese over the mixture and lets it melt. (5) She adds sesame oil and tears up pieces of Korean seaweed to sprinkle on top. (6) The final phase shows her tasting the completed dish with exaggerated expressions of delight and then holding up seaweed packets while gesturing toward the camera to promote them.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, high-tempo pop music with a strong electronic beat begins. A female voice is heard making satisfying eating and slurping sounds, followed by an enthusiastic "Yummy!" (2) The music continues as the female speaker provides a fast-paced narration in Chinese, describing the steps of making the porridge. (3) Sound effects like the crisp rustling of seaweed packets, the clicking of scissors, and the bubbling of the pot are layered over the music. (4) The speaker's tone becomes more persuasive and high-pitched during the final segment as she transitions from describing the food to discussing a product sale. (5) The video ends with a final upbeat musical flourish and the speaker's closing remarks.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Hook Text: "POV 當韓國人感冒時吃的起司粥🔥" (POV: Cheese porridge Koreans eat when they have a cold) - Tone: Intriguing. (2) Functional Text: "1. 加飯一起拌一拌" (1. Add rice and mix together) - Tone: Instructional. (3) Decorative Text: "我自己超愛吃辣...再加一包火辣雞醬🔥" (I personally love spicy... adding another pack of fire chicken sauce) - Tone: Personal/Enthusiastic. (4) Functional Text: "2. 灑上一點韓國黑胡椒粉" (2. Sprinkle on some Korean black pepper powder). (5) Functional Text: "3. 加披薩起司!!!" (3. Add pizza cheese!!!) - Tone: Excited. (6) Functional Text: "4. 關火後加一點香油" (4. After turning off the heat, add a bit of sesame oil). (7) Decorative Text: "完成" (Finished) and "起司的濃郁口感真的大爆發" (The rich texture of the cheese really explodes) - Tone: Satisfied. (8) Promotional Text: "韓國海苔最低價團購 08/23 - 08/27" (Korean seaweed lowest price group buy 08/23 - 08/27) - Tone: Urgency/Call to action.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro Hook -> Step-by-Step Cooking Demo -> Eating Reaction -> Commercial Endorsement

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 female creator present throughout the tutorial and tasting.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long brown hair, wearing a pink top in the intro/outro and a black-and-white checkered dress during the cooking segment.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A domestic dining setting with an electric cooking pot on a table, scattered food packaging, and a neutral-colored wall behind the creator.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Tutorial -> Review -> Call to Action
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is designed to promote a specific brand of Korean seaweed (Grandma's Onion Seaweed) through a limited-time group buy. It achieves this by first providing a high-value, relatable 'comfort food' cooking tutorial for 'Korean Cheese Porridge,' building audience engagement through food aesthetics and ASMR elements before concluding with a direct commercial offer.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct mention of a 'lowest price group buy' with specific dates and a call to action for viewers to comment to receive a purchase link.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Heavy use of variety-show style floating text and beat-synced editing to maintain high engagement.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video successfully merges the 'mukbang' and 'how-to' genres to create a high-conversion sales environment, leveraging cultural tropes of Korean comfort food.
